Equivalent (JA): 量と単位ドメインライブラリ
Definition (EN): The Quantities and Units Domain Library defines reusable and extensible model elements for physical quantities, including vector and tensor quantities, quantity dimensions, measurement units, measurement scales, coordinate frames, coordinate transformations and vector spaces. The library also enables the specification of coherent systems of quantities and systems of units, as well as the operators and functions needed to support quantity arithmetic in expressions.
Context/Example (EN): The Quantities and Units Domain Library defines reusable and extensible model elements for physical quantities, including vector and tensor quantities, quantity dimensions, measurement units, measurement scales, coordinate frames, coordinate transformations and vector spaces.

Definition (EN): The ISQ package specifies a complete set of predefined quantity types for the International System of Quantities (ISQ).
Context/Example (EN): The ISQ package specifies a complete set of predefined quantity types for the International System of Quantities (ISQ).

Definition (EN): This package specifies the measurement units as defined in ISO/IEC 80000 parts 3 to 13, the International System of (Measurement) Units -- Système International d'Unités (SI).
Context/Example (EN): This package specifies the measurement units as defined in ISO/IEC 80000 parts 3 to 13, the International System of (Measurement) Units -- Système International d'Unités (SI).

Definition (EN): This package specifies all US Customary measurement units, with conversion factors to compatible SI units, as defined in Appendix B "Conversion Factors" of [NIST SP-811].
Context/Example (EN): This package specifies all US Customary measurement units, with conversion factors to compatible SI units, as defined in Appendix B "Conversion Factors" of [NIST SP-811].

Definition (EN): A TensorQuantityValue is an abstract AttributeDefinition and a specialization of Collections::Array that represents a tensor quantity value as a sequence of Numbers and a TensorMeasurementReference.
Context/Example (EN): A TensorQuantityValue is an abstract AttributeDefinition and a specialization of Collections::Array that represents a tensor quantity value as a sequence of Numbers and a TensorMeasurementReference.

Definition (EN): A VectorQuantityValue is an AttributeDefinition that represents the value of a vector quantity by a tuple of Numbers and a VectorMeasurementReference. It is a specialization of TensorQuantityValue and has order one.
Context/Example (EN): A VectorQuantityValue is an AttributeDefinition that represents the value of a vector quantity by a tuple of Numbers and a VectorMeasurementReference. It is a specialization of TensorQuantityValue and has order one.

Definition (EN): A ScalarQuantityValue is an abstract AttributeDefinition that specializes VectorQuantityValue. It represents a scalar quantity value as a tuple of a Number num and a ScalarMeasurementReference mRef. By definition it has order zero.
Context/Example (EN): A ScalarQuantityValue is an abstract AttributeDefinition that specializes VectorQuantityValue.

Definition (EN): A QuantityDimension is the product of powers of the set of base quantities defined for a particular system of quantities, units and scales.
Context/Example (EN): A QuantityDimension is the product of powers of the set of base quantities defined for a particular system of quantities, units and scales.

Definition (EN): A QuantityPowerFactor is a representation of a quantity power factor, being the combination of a quantity and an exponent.
Context/Example (EN): A QuantityPowerFactor is a representation of a quantity power factor, being the combination of a quantity and an exponent.

Definition (EN): A SystemOfQuantities represents the essentials of [VIM] concept system of quantities (https://jcgm.bipm.org/vim/en/1.3.html), defined as a set of quantities together with a set of noncontradictory equations relating those quantities.
Context/Example (EN): A SystemOfQuantities represents the essentials of [VIM] concept system of quantities (https://jcgm.bipm.org/vim/en/1.3.html), defined as a set of quantities together with a set of noncontradictory equations relating those quantities.

Definition (EN): This package defines the general AttributeDefinitions and AttributeUsages to construct measurement references. This includes: measurement units, ordinal, logarithmic and cyclic measurement scales, unit conversions, coordinate frames and coordinate transformations, measurement unit prefixes to denote multiples (such as mega) and sub-multiples (such as nano).
Context/Example (EN): This package defines the general AttributeDefinitions and AttributeUsages to construct measurement references.

Definition (EN): A 3dCoordinateFrame is a CoordinateFrame that has dimensions.
Context/Example (EN): A 3dCoordinateFrame is a CoordinateFrame that has dimensions.

Definition (EN): AffineTransformationMatrix3d is a three dimensional CoordinateTransformation specified via an affine 4x4 transformation matrix
Context/Example (EN): AffineTransformationMatrix3d is a three dimensional CoordinateTransformation specified via an affine 4x4 transformation matrix

Definition (EN): ConversionByConvention is a UnitConversion that is defined according to some convention.
Context/Example (EN): ConversionByConvention is a UnitConversion that is defined according to some convention.

Definition (EN): ConversionByPrefix is a UnitConversion that is defined through reference to a named [ISO/IEC 80000-1] UnitPrefix, which represents a conversion factor that is a decimal or binary multiple or sub-multiple.
Context/Example (EN): ConversionByPrefix is a UnitConversion that is defined through reference to a named [ISO/IEC 80000-1] UnitPrefix, which represents a conversion factor that is a decimal or binary multiple or sub-multiple.

Definition (EN): A CoordinateFrame is a VectorMeasurementReference with the specific purpose to quantify (i.e., coordinatize) a vector space, and optionally locate and orient it with respect to another CoordinateFrame.
Context/Example (EN): A CoordinateFrame is a VectorMeasurementReference with the specific purpose to quantify (i.e., coordinatize) a vector space, and optionally locate and orient it with respect to another CoordinateFrame.

Definition (EN): CoordinateFramePlacement is a CoordinateTransformation by placement of the target frame in the source frame.
Context/Example (EN): CoordinateFramePlacement is a CoordinateTransformation by placement of the target frame in the source frame.

Definition (EN): A CoordinateTransformation is an AttributeDefinition that defines the transformation relationship between two coordinate systems, that are represented by VectorMeasurementReferences, typically CoordinateFrames.
Context/Example (EN): A CoordinateTransformation is an AttributeDefinition that defines the transformation relationship between two coordinate systems, that are represented by VectorMeasurementReferences, typically CoordinateFrames.

Definition (EN): CountValue is an explicit definition of a generic count quantity as a DimensionOneValue.
Context/Example (EN): CountValue is an explicit definition of a generic count quantity as a DimensionOneValue.

Definition (EN): CyclicRationScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a ratio scale with a periodic cycle.
Context/Example (EN): CyclicRationScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a ratio scale with a periodic cycle.

Definition (EN): A DefinitionalQuantityValue is a representation of a particular quantity value that is used in the definition of a MeasurementReference. Typically such a particular value is defined by convention.
Context/Example (EN): A DefinitionalQuantityValue is a representation of a particular quantity value that is used in the definition of a MeasurementReference.

Definition (EN): DerivedUnit is a MeasurementUnit that represents a measurement unit that depends on one or more powers of other measurement units.
Context/Example (EN): DerivedUnit is a MeasurementUnit that represents a measurement unit that depends on one or more powers of other measurement units.

Definition (EN): DimensionOneUnit is an explicit definition of unit of dimension one, also known as dimensionless unit.
Context/Example (EN): DimensionOneUnit is an explicit definition of unit of dimension one, also known as dimensionless unit.

Definition (EN): A DimensionOneValue is a ScalarQuantityValue with a DimensionOneUnit.
Context/Example (EN): A DimensionOneValue is a ScalarQuantityValue with a DimensionOneUnit.

Definition (EN): IntervalScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a linear interval measurement scale, i.e. a scale on which only intervals between two values are meaningful and not their ratios.
Context/Example (EN): IntervalScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a linear interval measurement scale, i.e. a scale on which only intervals between two values are meaningful and not their ratios.

Definition (EN): LogarithmicScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a logarithmic measurement scale that is defined as follows. The numeric value v of a ratio quantity expressed on a logarithmic scale equivalent with a value x of the same quantity expressed on a ratio scale (i.e. only using a MeasurementUnit) is computed as follows: \(v = f \cdot \log_b (x/x_{ref})^a \).
Context/Example (EN): LogarithmicScale is a MeasurementScale that represents a logarithmic measurement scale that is defined as follows. The numeric value v of a ratio quantity expressed on a logarithmic scale equivalent with a value x of the same quantity expressed on a ratio scale (i.e. only using a MeasurementUnit) is computed as follows: \(v = f \cdot \log_b (x/x_{ref})^a \).

Definition (EN): MeasurementScale is a MeasurementReference that represents a measurement scale.
Context/Example (EN): MeasurementScale is a MeasurementReference that represents a measurement scale.

Definition (EN): A MeasurementUnit is a ScalarMeasurementReference that represents a measurement unit. As defined in [VIM] a measurement unit is a real scalar quantity, defined and adopted by convention, with which any other quantity of the same kind can be compared to express the ratio of the two quantities as a number.
Context/Example (EN): A MeasurementUnit is a ScalarMeasurementReference that represents a measurement unit.

Definition (EN): NullTransformation is an AffineTransformationMatrix3d that places the target CoordinateFrame at the same position and orientation as the source CoordinateFrame.
Context/Example (EN): NullTransformation is an AffineTransformationMatrix3d that places the target CoordinateFrame at the same position and orientation as the source CoordinateFrame.

Definition (EN): An OrdinalScale is a MeasurementScale that represents an ordinal measurement scale, i.e. a scale on which only the ordering of quantity values is meaningful, not the intervals between any pair of values and neither their ratio.
Context/Example (EN): An OrdinalScale is a MeasurementScale that represents an ordinal measurement scale, i.e. a scale on which only the ordering of quantity values is meaningful, not the intervals between any pair of values and neither their ratio.

Definition (EN): QuantityValueMapping is an AttributeDefinition that represents the mapping of equivalent quantity values expressed on two different measurement scales.
Context/Example (EN): QuantityValueMapping is an AttributeDefinition that represents the mapping of equivalent quantity values expressed on two different measurement scales.

Definition (EN): Representation of a rotation about an axis over an angle.
Context/Example (EN): Representation of a rotation about an axis over an angle.

Definition (EN): A ScalarMeasurementReference is a specialization of VectorMeasurementReference. It represents a single measurement reference for a ScalarQuantityValue or for a component of a tensor or vector quantity.
Context/Example (EN): A ScalarMeasurementReference is a specialization of VectorMeasurementReference.

Definition (EN): SimpleUnit is a MeasurementUnit that does not depend on any other measurement unit.
Context/Example (EN): SimpleUnit is a MeasurementUnit that does not depend on any other measurement unit.

Definition (EN): A SystemOfUnits represents the essentials of [VIM] concept system of units (https://jcgm.bipm.org/vim/en/1.13.html), defined as a set of base units and derived units, together with their multiples and submultiples, defined in accordance with given rules, for a given system of quantities.
Context/Example (EN): A SystemOfUnits represents the essentials of [VIM] concept system of units (https://jcgm.bipm.org/vim/en/1.13.html), defined as a set of base units and derived units, together with their multiples and submultiples, defined in accordance with given rules, for a given system of quantities.

Definition (EN): A TensorMeasurementReference is an abstract AttributeDefinition and a specialization of Collections::Array, that represents the [VIM] concept measurement reference, but generalized for tensor, vector and scalar quantities.
Context/Example (EN): A TensorMeasurementReference is an abstract AttributeDefinition and a specialization of Collections::Array, that represents the [VIM] concept measurement reference, but generalized for tensor, vector and scalar quantities.

Definition (EN): Representation of a translation with respect to a coordinate frame.
Context/Example (EN): Representation of a translation with respect to a coordinate frame.

Definition (EN): TranslationOrRotation is an abstract union of Translation and Rotation
Context/Example (EN): TranslationOrRotation is an abstract union of Translation and Rotation

Definition (EN): A TranslationRotationSequence is a coordinate frame transformation specified by a sequence of translations and/or rotations.
Context/Example (EN): A TranslationRotationSequence is a coordinate frame transformation specified by a sequence of translations and/or rotations.

Definition (EN): UnitConversion is an AttributeDefinition that represents a linear conversion relationship between one measurement unit and another measurement unit, that acts as a reference.
Context/Example (EN): UnitConversion is an AttributeDefinition that represents a linear conversion relationship between one measurement unit and another measurement unit, that acts as a reference.

Definition (EN): UnitPowerFactor is an AttributeDefinition that represents a power factor of a MeasurementUnit and an exponent.
Context/Example (EN): UnitPowerFactor is an AttributeDefinition that represents a power factor of a MeasurementUnit and an exponent.

Definition (EN): UnitPrefix is an AttributeDefinition that represents a named multiple or sub-multiple measurement unit prefix as defined in ISO/IEC 80000-1.
Context/Example (EN): UnitPrefix is an AttributeDefinition that represents a named multiple or sub-multiple measurement unit prefix as defined in ISO/IEC 80000-1.

Definition (EN): A VectorMeasurementReference is a specialization of TensorMeasurementReference for vector quantities that are typed by a VectorQuantityValue. Its order is typically one, but can be zero in case of a specialization to ScalarMeasurementReference.
Context/Example (EN): A VectorMeasurementReference is a specialization of TensorMeasurementReference for vector quantities that are typed by a VectorQuantityValue.

Definition (EN): Construction of a literal or variable ScalarQuantityValue is done through the [ operator, matched by a closing ]. The signature of the corresponding CalculationDefinition is: calc def '[' specializes BaseFunctions::'[' { in num: Number[1]; in mRef: ScalarMeasurementReference[1]; return quantity : ScalarQuantityValue[1]; }
Context/Example (EN): Construction of a literal or variable ScalarQuantityValue is done through the [ operator, matched by a closing ].

Definition (EN): Construction of a literal or variable VectorQuantityValue is done through the [ operator, matched by a closing ]. The signature of the corresponding CalculationDefinition is: calc def '[' specializes BaseFunctions::'[' { in num : Number[1..n]; // a sequence of numbers that are the numerical values of the vector in mRef : VectorMeasurementReference[1]; return quantity : VectorQuantityValue[1]; private attribute n = mRef.flattenedSize; }
Context/Example (EN): Construction of a literal or variable VectorQuantityValue is done through the [ operator, matched by a closing ].

Context/Example (EN): Construction of a literal or variable TensorQuantityValue is done through the [ operator, matched by a closing ].
